rulings.Case Title: JAMES OWEN et al VS. ASBESTOS DFDTS.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 26, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Pacific Bell Telephone Company's motion for summary judgment will be heard at 9:30a.m. in Department 503, Hon. Richard B. Ulmer presiding. Judge Wong is recusing himself. Defendant's motion is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a), 1010.6, 12a and 12c. Plaintiffs' objection based on timeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/RBU)

On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 26, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Simcic was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Simcic's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Even if defendant did sustain its initial burden, Mr. Simcic's deposition testimony and declaration, the deposition testimony of James Reichert and defendant's responses to standard interrogatories create a triable issue whether Mr. Simcic was exposed to asbestos-containing cement pipe attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to properly raise the issue whether exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Simcic's increased risk of developing his asbestos-related illness. Plaintiffs' objections to defendant's responses to request for production (Exhibit 34) are sustained. Defendant's objections to Mr. Simcic's declaration and Mr. Reichert's deposition testimony are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: ABRAHAM CASTILLO ET AL VS. CLEAVER-BROOKS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 26, 2016 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion to transfer venue to Riverside County is denied. The motion is denied on the proper court ground because it is untimely. Defendant filed an answer and did not file a motion to transfer at that time. (C.C.P. §§ 396b(a), 397(a).) Even if the Court could consider a discretionary transfer pursuant to C.C.P. § 397(a), defendant failed to meet its burden of demonstrating that San Francisco is not the proper court. Finally, even assuming San Francisco is not the proper court, defendant may only move to change venue to its residence and defendant did not show that its residence is in Riverside County. As to C.C.P. § 397(c), defendant failed to meet its burden of demonstrating that the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice will be promoted by transfer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
